questions.text: I am attuned to the way social norms shift from one context to another within the same group.
I invest time in building habits that will pay dividends over months and years.
I believe that resources are meant to be used in service of present opportunities.
I believe that adaptability is one of my core strengths.
I believe that the moral priority of suffering is the insight that reveals whether a community's stated values are real or decorative.
I believe that the moral weight of suffering is what keeps ethics rooted in human reality rather than floating in abstraction.
I believe that the ability to process emotional situations without being overwhelmed by them is a cognitive achievement that supports both personal well-being and public service.
When I witness something moving, my eyes well up before I've even decided how I feel about it.
I feel most alive when I am working toward my own aspirations.
I feel that my directness helps groups move forward.
I do not naturally sense when a room is tense.
I routinely pick up tasks no one else wants to do, so the group can function well.
I instinctively delegate tasks and coordinate efforts.
I tend to feel the same emotional temperature whether I'm at home or in a novel situation.
I find that writing things down helps me commit to them and hold myself accountable.
I often sense the unspoken expectations in a social situation.
I go from calm to fully activated in a matter of seconds.
I routinely make financial sacrifices to support people in my life.
